title: Kaiana Bolton’s CV
text:
Aspiring Accountant and Future CPA

isaac@applesdofall.org | My wikipedia page  | Download PDF



Full time student at BYU-Idaho. Anticipated graduation date: July 2026.


Excel, IDEA, Altryx, and Other Audit documentation software.




Graduated May 2021 Aberdeen High School

Valedictorian.
Honors Society President for 2020-2021.

August 2021 - June 2024 Idaho State University

Bachelor of Science in Accounting.
Student Volunteer, ISU Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA).

September 2024 - Present Brigham Young University-Idaho

Bachelor of Science in Accounting.
Certificate in Data Science.




Janruary 2025 - April 2025 WSRP-Now Richey May Utah, Audit Intern, Salt Lake City, UT

Collaborated in planning and executing financial audits, including substantive testing, analytical procedures, and internal control evaluations for clients across various industries.
Analyzed financial statements, tested transactions, and evaluated internal controls to support compliance with GAAP and regulatory standards.
Prepared workpapers, documented audit findings, and collaborated with senior auditors on risk assessments.
Utilized audit software and Excel to prepare workpapers, review supporting documentation, and assist with testing procedures.

October 2022 - August 2023 Idaho Central Credit Union, Customer Service Specialist, Chubbuck, ID

Communicated with members to resolve account concerns, maintain relationships, and deliver high-quality customer service.
Selected for a specialized team to handle escalated member disputes and provide timely issue resolution.

September 2021 - October 2021 Thompson Farms, Truck Driver, Pinegree, ID

Operated a 10-wheel truck during potato and beet harvest to transport crops efficiently and safely.
Transferred harvested goods from fields to storage facilities to support time-sensitive harvest operations.

September 2020 - May 2021 Wallace Drug, Retail Clerk/Cashier, Aberdeen, ID

Processed customer transactions for purchases, returns, and exchanges accurately and efficiently.
Answered incoming calls and directed customers to correct department or staff member.
Maintained store organization by cleaning, stocking shelves, and collaborating with team members to support daily operations.

September 2011 - October 2019 COMA Farms, Laborer, Aberdeen, ID

Worked in seasonal potato harvests for eight consecutive years, contributing approximately two and a half weeks each season.
Operated potato piler equipment for harvest and sorting operations.
